sources.title: Why Simple Mechanics Make Addictive Games
Why Clicker Games Are So Popular
Psychology of Rewards in Modern Games
Engineered Highs: Reward Variability and Frequency as Potential Prerequisites of Behavioural Addiction
Video Game Training and the Reward System
Gamification in Medical Education: Retention and Engagement (Randomized Trial)
Using Spaced Repetition and Gamification to Enhance K-12 Student Science Literacy

mechanics.name: Instant Feedback
mechanics.description: Visual effects, sound cues, and number changes within milliseconds of player action
mechanics.why: Triggers immediate dopamine release in ventral striatum. Every action must produce a visible/audible reaction.
mechanics.learning_application: Correct = satisfying animation + sound; wrong = shake + show correct answer immediately

mechanics.name: Variable Reward Schedules
mechanics.description: Unpredictable rewards given at non-fixed intervals or magnitudes
mechanics.why: Anticipation of a possible reward triggers more dopamine than a guaranteed reward (Skinner's operant conditioning). Variable reinforcement schedules are the most resistant to extinction.
mechanics.learning_application: Random bonus points, surprise streak multipliers, occasional 'bonus round' questions

mechanics.name: Visible Progression
mechanics.description: Progress bars, level indicators, unlockable tiers that show advancement toward a goal
mechanics.why: The 'goal gradient effect' — motivation increases as you approach a milestone. The next level always feels just out of reach.
mechanics.learning_application: Per-topic mastery meter tied to spaced repetition decay model. Visible comprehension score per airspace class.

mechanics.name: Low Effort / High Reward Ratio
mechanics.description: Minimal cognitive or motor cost to perform an action, but disproportionately satisfying feedback
mechanics.why: Removes friction between intention and action. The lower the barrier to act, the higher the repetition rate.
mechanics.learning_application: Tap-to-select or drag-to-match interactions, not free-text entry. Reduce input friction.

mechanics.name: Time Pressure
mechanics.description: Countdown timers or speed-based scoring that create urgency
mechanics.why: Activates focus and flow state. Perceived scarcity of time increases perceived value of correct action.
mechanics.learning_application: Optional timed mode for review sessions. Bonus points for fast correct answers.

mechanics.name: Social Comparison
mechanics.description: Leaderboards, head-to-head competition, or shared progress visibility
mechanics.why: Competitiveness increases attention and engagement. Social presence raises stakes without adding content complexity.
mechanics.learning_application: Compare mastery scores with study partners. Could enable parent-child or student-student competition.

mechanics.name: Pattern Matching / Categorization
mechanics.description: Matching, sorting, or categorizing items into groups
mechanics.why: Taps into innate pattern recognition circuitry. Clear success/failure signal. Low cognitive overhead.
mechanics.learning_application: Match airspace class to its attributes. Sort requirements into correct airspace bucket. Drag-drop categorization.

key_findings.spaced_repetition_plus_gamification: ~300% better retention vs traditional learning; ~200% from spaced repetition alone (eLearning Industry analysis of 50,000+ users)
key_findings.dopamine_and_games: Video gaming associated with endogenous dopamine release in ventral striatum (Frontiers in Human Neuroscience)
key_findings.variable_vs_fixed_rewards: Variable reinforcement schedules produce more persistent engagement than fixed schedules (ScienceDirect, Skinner)
key_findings.quiz_plus_challenge: Studies combining assessment with conflict/challenge attributes showed increased use of learning material and improved outcomes (PMC systematic review)

for_learning_looper: Use tap/drag-drop interactions, not free-text input — minimize friction
Every answer (right or wrong) must produce immediate visual + audio feedback
Add variable bonus mechanics: random streak multipliers, surprise bonus rounds
Show per-topic mastery meter tied to spaced repetition decay curve
Offer optional timed mode for advanced review
Support match-class-to-attribute and sort-into-category question formats
Consider social/competitive features for study partners
